commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6045507ec501f5b9d8cb4bf9cd587855c08fbd21)
commands.review_summary:
The PR widens `sessions_spawn.taskName` validation, schema text, and regression tests to accept lowercase hyphenated aliases while preserving underscores and invalid-name rejection.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +21. Total +21 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection is enough to reproduce the current behavior: current `main` rejects hyphenated `taskName` values through the central regex before spawn. I did not run a live session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human/contributor follow-up is needed because external real behavior proof must come from the contributor's setup; automation should not repair the proof gate for them.

target.title: fix: allow hyphenated subagent task names
target.body:
Summary
- Allow `sessions_spawn.taskName` to use hyphenated lowercase slugs such as `code-review`.
- Keep the existing lowercase-first validation contract and continue rejecting uppercase, spaces, leading hyphens, and empty values.
- Align the tool schema description and regression tests with the accepted task-name grammar.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn.taskName` accepts common hyphenated task slugs while preserving rejection for invalid names.
Real environment tested: local upstream/main-based worktree under `.claude/worktrees/fix-subagent-taskname-hyphen`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.
Evidence after fix: the sessions spawn tests cover hyphenated names, underscore aliases, and invalid uppercase/space/leading-hyphen inputs.
Observed result after fix: Vitest passed with 2 test files and 98 tests; diff whitespace check was clean.
What was not tested: no live subagent session was launched outside the unit test harness.
target.author: lanzhi-lee

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
